concept: Plateau / High Flat Land / Elevated Plain
description: An area of relatively level high ground. 'Shupi' names the elevated flats—where land rises to create high plains, offering both the openness of lowlands and the perspective of heights.
sound_symbolism: The word rises to level height. 'Shu' /ʃu/ lifts with soft breath, the ascent. Then 'pi' flattens at the top, the level high ground.
grammatical_notes: Functions as a noun. Plural with 'lo': 'lo shupi' (plateaus). A plateau is a landform rather than a detached or crafted object, so it has no canonical classifier. Combines qualities of 'wemo' (plain) and 'tomora' (mountain)—high yet flat.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Plateaus teach stability at height—having risen, one finds level ground, perspective without continued striving.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: High plateaus offered unique ecosystems and refuge—places where communities found both isolation and prospect.
